title: C Pointers
content:
### Pointers in C

A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable.

### Pointer Declaration

```c
type *pointerName;
```

### Key Operators

| Operator | Name | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `&` | Address-of | Gets the memory address of a variable |
| `*` | Dereference | Accesses the value at the stored address |

### Example

```c
int x = 10;
int *p = &x;  // p stores address of x
*p = 20;      // changes x to 20
```

### Pointer Size

All pointers on a given system have the same size (4 bytes on 32-bit, 8 bytes on 64-bit), regardless of the type they point to.

### Null Pointers

```c
int *p = NULL;  // points to nothing
```

A null pointer should not be dereferenced. Always check for NULL before using a pointer.

---

> **Key Point:** A pointer must be initialized before use. Dereferencing an uninitialized pointer causes undefined behavior (likely a crash).

quiz.question: What operator is used to get the address of a variable?
